Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- cma-doctor: Validate Concept Model Kit installation
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- cma-normalize: Apply whitespace normalization to concept model documents
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-validate: Validate concept model links and annotations
- cma-render: Transform concept model to ClaudeMark format
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Docket:
  ## Character
  
  Rust code wiring the existing Clipbuddy focus handler to the container's bind-mount interface. Not architectural — APCS0 and APCPS already pin every contract this pace consumes. Sonnet-scale, mostly mechanical plumbing with one interesting beat (graceful degradation when the container isn't up).
  
  ## Prerequisites
  
  ₢A9AAZ (`container-scaffolding`) must land first — the container has to exist and produce valid `{N}.json` before Rust can integrate with it. Smoke-test validation of the container is the entry gate for this pace.
  
  ## Context
  
  A9AAV decided: Rust Clipbuddy stays the frontend; sophisticated algorithms live in the container; combining stays in Rust (strongly typed). ₢A9AAZ constructs the container in isolation. This pace builds the Rust half: write `{N}-in.txt`, watch for `{N}.json`, deserialize, log. No combining yet — this pace proves the loop, the next pace (combining-v1, deferred) does the intelligence once empirical evidence is in hand.
  
  ## Scope — Harvest Update
  
  Update `apcrh_harvest.rs`:
  
  - On the Clinical branch, after the existing raw-flavor captures (`{N}-in.clinical.{ext}` family), also write `$HOME/apcjd/{N}-in.txt` containing `apcs_normalized_text`: plain UTF-8 output of the existing HTML-strip / whitespace-collapse / encoding-normalization path.
  - The bare `{N}-in.txt` name (no `{tag}` infix, no UTI suffix) is documented in APCPS Clipboard Harvest section as the container's input file.
  - Non-clinical branch still writes only raw captures; no `{N}-in.txt` on that branch (no detection pipeline runs).
  - Write failure is non-fatal: log via `apcrl_error_now!` and continue. Matches the existing harvest failure-mode discipline.
  
  Update `apcth_harvest.rs`:
  
  - Add tests covering `{N}-in.txt` write on Clinical branch.
  - Confirm the scanner's leading-digit parse still handles the expanded file family correctly.
  
  ## Scope — Container Integration Module
  
  Create `apcd/src/apcrc_container.rs`:
  
  - Rust types mirroring the APCPS JSON schemas:
    - `ApcrcStanfordFinding { text, start, end, label, confidence }`
    - `ApcrcSpacyFinding` — enum over `Token { text, start, end, pos, tag, morph, lemma, head, dep }` and `Entity { text, start, end, label }` variants discriminated by the `kind` field.
    - `ApcrcStanzaFinding` — same pattern, `Token { text, start, end, upos, xpos, feats, lemma, head, deprel, ner }` and `Entity { text, start, end, label }`.
    - `ApcrcContainerOutput { index, stanford: { findings: Vec<Stanford> }, spacy: { findings: Vec<Spacy> }, stanza: { findings: Vec<Stanza> } }` at top level.
  - `serde::Deserialize` derives on all types. Use `#[serde(tag = "kind")]` for the token/entity enums.
  - Function `apcrc_await_output(journal_dir: &Path, index: u64, timeout: Duration) -> Result<ApcrcContainerOutput>` that uses the `notify` crate (FSEvents on macOS) to watch for `{index}.json` existing, then reads and deserializes. Returns an informative error on timeout or on deserialize failure.
  
  Create `apcd/src/apctc_container.rs`:
  
  - Tests against fixture JSON strings matching the APCPS schema for each discerner variant — token + entity, each with representative field values. Confirm deserialization produces the expected Rust values.
  - Add a golden-file test: a small fixture `{N}.json` on disk, `apcrc_await_output` picks it up and deserializes correctly.
  
  Wire into `lib.rs`:
  
  - `pub mod apcrc_container;`
  - `#[cfg(test)] mod apctc_container;`
  
  ## Scope — Focus Handler Wire-up
  
  Update `apcap_main.rs::zapcap_handle_focus`:
  
  - On Clinical branch, after the existing harvest + anonymized-output write path, call `apcrc_await_output(journal_dir, N, Duration::from_secs(30))`.
  - On success: log `[INFO] container output received: stanford={S} spacy={C} stanza={Z}` where the three counts are the `findings.len()` from each discerner key. No combining, no UI consumption — just the log line confirming the loop works.
  - On timeout or error: log `[WARN] container output not received within 30s for index N: {error}`. Focus handler continues normally — existing Rust-only triage path is authoritative for UI.
  - The graceful-degradation discipline is load-bearing: if the container is down, triage still works with Rust-side findings only.
  
  ## Scope — Documentation Cascade
  
  - `apck-claude-context.md`: add `apcrc_container.rs` + `apctc_container.rs` rows to the Source modules table. Update the Detection Architecture section if the integration point warrants a note.
  - APCPS: minor tightening if any reality diverges from the JSON schema as written; spec is master, Rust types must match it.
  
  ## Constraints
  
  - **No combining logic this pace**. Container findings accumulate in the log, not in the evidence pool yet. Combining is a later pace with empirical input.
  - **Graceful degradation is non-negotiable**: the app must start and function if the container is not running. Ann should not be blocked by a missing or crashed container.
  - **Timeout budget**: 30s is a placeholder — adjust based on observed container latency during smoke testing.
  
  ## Exit Criteria
  
  - `tt/apcw-t.Test.sh` passes including new `apctc_container` tests. `deny(warnings)` holds.
  - `tt/apcw-fl.FixtureLoad.sh` followed by focus, with the container running, produces:
    - `$HOME/apcjd/{N}-in.txt` with normalized text
    - `$HOME/apcjd/{N}.json` from the container (within 30s)
    - `apcap.log` line: `container output received: stanford=S spacy=C stanza=Z`
  - Same fixture load with the container **stopped** produces a graceful timeout warning in `apcap.log` and normal triage behavior in the UI.
  - `apck-claude-context.md` source modules table includes `apcrc` / `apctc` entries.
  
  ## Out of Scope
  
  - Combining rules — deferred to a later pace once empirical data from this integration is available.
  - UI consumption of container findings — container findings are log-only this pace.
  - Decomposing the existing Rust-side `apcrm_match.rs` into five separate scans to match APCS0 — that's a refactoring pace, orthogonal from this integration, can be deferred until combining is spec'd.
  - Performance tuning of the container roundtrip.
  - Anonymized-output (`{N}-out.txt`) awareness of container findings — still derives from Rust-only findings until combining exists.

Docket:
  ## Character
  
  Rust code wiring the existing Clipbuddy focus handler to the container's bind-mount interface. Not architectural — APCS0 and APCPS already pin every contract this pace consumes. Sonnet-scale, mostly mechanical plumbing with one interesting beat (graceful degradation when the container isn't up).
  
  ## Prerequisites
  
  ₢A9AAZ (`container-scaffolding`) must land first — the container has to exist and produce valid `{N}.json` before Rust can integrate with it. Smoke-test validation of the container is the entry gate for this pace.
  
  ## Context
  
  A9AAV decided: Rust Clipbuddy stays the frontend; sophisticated algorithms live in the container; combining stays in Rust (strongly typed). ₢A9AAZ constructs the container in isolation. This pace builds the Rust half: write `{N}-in.txt`, watch for `{N}.json`, deserialize, log. No combining yet — this pace proves the loop, the next pace (combining-v1, deferred) does the intelligence once empirical evidence is in hand.
  
  ## Scope — Harvest Update
  
  Update `apcrh_harvest.rs`:
  
  - On the Clinical branch, after the existing raw-flavor captures (`{N}-in.clinical.{ext}` family), also write `$HOME/apcjd/{N}-in.txt` containing `apcs_normalized_text`: plain UTF-8 output of the existing HTML-strip / whitespace-collapse / encoding-normalization path.
  - The bare `{N}-in.txt` name (no `{tag}` infix, no UTI suffix) is documented in APCPS Clipboard Harvest section as the container's input file.
  - Non-clinical branch still writes only raw captures; no `{N}-in.txt` on that branch (no detection pipeline runs).
  - Write failure is non-fatal: log via `apcrl_error_now!` and continue. Matches the existing harvest failure-mode discipline.
  
  Update `apcth_harvest.rs`:
  
  - Add tests covering `{N}-in.txt` write on Clinical branch.
  - Confirm the scanner's leading-digit parse still handles the expanded file family correctly.
  
  ## Scope — Container Integration Module
  
  Create `apcd/src/apcrc_container.rs`:
  
  - Rust types mirroring the APCPS JSON schemas:
    - `ApcrcStanfordFinding { text, start, end, label, confidence }`
    - `ApcrcSpacyFinding` — enum over `Token { text, start, end, pos, tag, morph, lemma, head, dep }` and `Entity { text, start, end, label }` variants discriminated by the `kind` field.
    - `ApcrcStanzaFinding` — same pattern, `Token { text, start, end, upos, xpos, feats, lemma, head, deprel, ner }` and `Entity { text, start, end, label }`.
    - `ApcrcContainerOutput { index, stanford: { findings: Vec<Stanford> }, spacy: { findings: Vec<Spacy> }, stanza: { findings: Vec<Stanza> } }` at top level.
  - `serde::Deserialize` derives on all types. Use `#[serde(tag = "kind")]` for the token/entity enums.
  - Function `apcrc_await_output(journal_dir: &Path, index: u64, timeout: Duration) -> Result<ApcrcContainerOutput>` that uses the `notify` crate (FSEvents on macOS) to watch for `{index}.json` existing, then reads and deserializes. Returns an informative error on timeout or on deserialize failure.
  
  Create `apcd/src/apctc_container.rs`:
  
  - Tests against fixture JSON strings matching the APCPS schema for each discerner variant — token + entity, each with representative field values. Confirm deserialization produces the expected Rust values.
  - Add a golden-file test: a small fixture `{N}.json` on disk, `apcrc_await_output` picks it up and deserializes correctly.
  
  Wire into `lib.rs`:
  
  - `pub mod apcrc_container;`
  - `#[cfg(test)] mod apctc_container;`
  
  ## Scope — Focus Handler Wire-up
  
  Update `apcap_main.rs::zapcap_handle_focus`:
  
  - On Clinical branch, after the existing harvest + anonymized-output write path, call `apcrc_await_output(journal_dir, N, Duration::from_secs(30))`.
  - On success: log `[INFO] container output received: stanford={S} spacy={C} stanza={Z}` where the three counts are the `findings.len()` from each discerner key. No combining, no UI consumption — just the log line confirming the loop works.
  - On timeout or error: log `[WARN] container output not received within 30s for index N: {error}`. Focus handler continues normally — existing Rust-only triage path is authoritative for UI.
  - The graceful-degradation discipline is load-bearing: if the container is down, triage still works with Rust-side findings only.
  
  ## Scope — Documentation Cascade
  
  - `apck-claude-context.md`: add `apcrc_container.rs` + `apctc_container.rs` rows to the Source modules table. Update the Detection Architecture section if the integration point warrants a note.
  - APCPS: minor tightening if any reality diverges from the JSON schema as written; spec is master, Rust types must match it.
  
  ## Constraints
  
  - **No combining logic this pace**. Container findings accumulate in the log, not in the evidence pool yet. Combining is a later pace with empirical input.
  - **Graceful degradation is non-negotiable**: the app must start and function if the container is not running. Ann should not be blocked by a missing or crashed container.
  - **Timeout budget**: 30s is a placeholder — adjust based on observed container latency during smoke testing.
  
  ## Exit Criteria
  
  - `tt/apcw-t.Test.sh` passes including new `apctc_container` tests. `deny(warnings)` holds.
  - `tt/apcw-fl.FixtureLoad.sh` followed by focus, with the container running, produces:
    - `$HOME/apcjd/{N}-in.txt` with normalized text
    - `$HOME/apcjd/{N}.json` from the container (within 30s)
    - `apcap.log` line: `container output received: stanford=S spacy=C stanza=Z`
  - Same fixture load with the container **stopped** produces a graceful timeout warning in `apcap.log` and normal triage behavior in the UI.
  - `apck-claude-context.md` source modules table includes `apcrc` / `apctc` entries.
  
  ## Out of Scope
  
  - Combining rules — deferred to a later pace once empirical data from this integration is available.
  - UI consumption of container findings — container findings are log-only this pace.
  - Decomposing the existing Rust-side `apcrm_match.rs` into five separate scans to match APCS0 — that's a refactoring pace, orthogonal from this integration, can be deferred until combining is spec'd.
  - Performance tuning of the container roundtrip.
  - Anonymized-output (`{N}-out.txt`) awareness of container findings — still derives from Rust-only findings until combining exists.

1	# jjezs_paddock ₣A9
2	
3	## Character
4	
5	Product design into prototype implementation. Starts with synthesis (specs capturing a rich design conversation), transitions to exploratory engineering (Tauri + Rust + cross-platform clipboard), then steady implementation of a three-tier PHI detection engine. Requires careful attention to minting discipline — this kit lives alongside RBK/JJK/BUK and must respect the prefix universe.
6	
7	## Problem
8	
9	Clinicians using Epic EHR need to consult Open Evidence (an AI diagnostic tool) but must not paste Protected Health Information (PHI) into it. HIPAA Safe Harbor defines 18 PHI categories that must be removed. Today, clinicians either skip the consultation or manually scrub text — both are unacceptable. No clipboard-level tool exists to automate the triage.
10	
11	## Solution
12	
13	Ann's PHI Clipbuddy (`apcap`) — a Tauri desktop app (macOS, later Windows) that:
14	1. Validates clipboard content as clinical via heuristic, clears system clipboard after consumption
15	2. Parses Epic "Copy All" HTML for structural context (labels, sections)
16	3. Runs a three-tier PHI detection engine (regex → label-anchored → dictionary)
17	4. Displays a triage view: RED (definite PHI), YELLOW (questionable), GREY (pass)
18	5. Lets clinician toggle findings — YELLOW easily, RED with deliberate friction (separate panel section)
19	6. Writes anonymized plain text to clipboard with category-specific placeholders (DOB→age, >89→"90+")
20	7. Self-updates from `/Users/Shared/apcua/` without confirmation
21	
22	## Key Design Decisions
23	
24	- **No JavaScript**: All rendering logic is Rust. The webview is a passive display surface. Decided during ₢A9AAP after the byte-vs-char offset mismatch bug (₢A9AAK) demonstrated the cost of a two-language boundary in a HIPAA-adjacent tool. Policy documented in APCPS scope boundaries, APCAS product vision, and apck-claude-context.md.
25	- **Anchored vs Matched**: Tier 2 (label position) and Tier 3 (dictionary identity) are independent mechanisms with distinct vocabulary — see APCPS
26	- **Case-insensitive**: All dictionary lookups normalize to lowercase; original case preserved in display
27	- **Per-word anchoring**: No multi-word merge — each word individually classified
28	- **Conservative bias**: False positive preferred over false negative
29	- **Provider/facility names**: Treated as PHI (re-identifying in combination with clinical details)
30	- **Clipboard always cleared** after consumption
31	- **Clinical content heuristic** gates processing; non-clinical clipboard shows diagnostic and clears triage
32	- **Self-update watcher dormant for prototype** (₢A9AAS): `apcru_start_watcher()` is commented out in `apcap_main.rs::main()`; `apcru_update.rs` source retained for trivial revert. Ann's deploy ceremony is manual quit + relaunch of `Apcap.app` from `/Users/Shared/apcua/`. The seamless hot-swap-during-active-session problem is not one Ann has today. `tt/apcw-D.Deploy.sh` emits a `=== Forward to Ann ===` … `=== End ===` block with the launch instructions after each successful deploy — single source of truth for the ceremony.
33	
34	## Authoritative Documents
35	
36	| Document | Owns | Path |
37	|----------|------|------|
38	| Application Spec (APCAS) | UX plan, workflow, deployment, HIPAA context | `Tools/apck/APCAS-Specification.md` |
39	| Prototype Spec (APCPS) | Detection pipeline, tech stack, dictionaries, project structure, scope boundaries | `Tools/apck/APCPS-PrototypeSpecification.md` |
40	| Claude Context | Build/run/deploy instructions, file mappings, prefix tree, tabtargets | `Tools/apck/apck-claude-context.md` |
41	| Rust Coding Guide (RCG) | Naming conventions, test patterns, disciplines | `Tools/vok/vov_veiled/RCG-RustCodingGuide.md` |
42

46

74	
75	- Rust types mirroring the APCPS JSON schemas:
76	  - `ApcrcStanfordFinding { text, start, end, label, confidence }`
77	  - `ApcrcSpacyFinding` — enum over `Token { text, start, end, pos, tag, morph, lemma, head, dep }` and `Entity { text, start, end, label }` variants discriminated by the `kind` field.
78	  - `ApcrcStanzaFinding` — same pattern, `Token { text, start, end, upos, xpos, feats, lemma, head, deprel, ner }` and `Entity { text, start, end, label }`.
79	  - `ApcrcContainerOutput { index, stanford: { findings: Vec<Stanford> }, spacy: { findings: Vec<Spacy> }, stanza: { findings: Vec<Stanza> } }` at top level.
80	- `serde::Deserialize` derives on all types. Use `#[serde(tag = "kind")]` for the token/entity enums.
81	- Function `apcrc_await_output(journal_dir: &Path, index: u64, timeout: Duration) -> Result<ApcrcContainerOutput>` that uses the `notify` crate (FSEvents on macOS) to watch for `{index}.json` existing, then reads and deserializes. Returns an informative error on timeout or on deserialize failure.
82	
83	Create `apcd/src/apctc_container.rs`:
84	
85	- Tests against fixture JSON strings matching the APCPS schema for each discerner variant — token + entity, each with representative field values. Confirm deserialization produces the expected Rust values.
86	- Add a golden-file test: a small fixture `{N}.json` on disk, `apcrc_await_output` picks it up and deserializes correctly.
87	
88	Wire into `lib.rs`:
89	
90	- `pub mod apcrc_container;`
91	- `#[cfg(test)] mod apctc_container;`
92	
93	## Scope — Focus Handler Wire-up
94	
95	Update `apcap_main.rs::zapcap_handle_focus`:
96	
97	- On Clinical branch, after the existing harvest + anonymized-output write path, call `apcrc_await_output(journal_dir, N, Duration::from_secs(30))`.
98	- On success: log `[INFO] container output received: stanford={S} spacy={C} stanza={Z}` where the three counts are the `findings.len()` from each discerner key. No combining, no UI consumption — just the log line confirming the loop works.
99	- On timeout or error: log `[WARN] container output not received within 30s for index N: {error}`. Focus handler continues normally — existing Rust-only triage path is authoritative for UI.
100	- The graceful-degradation discipline is load-bearing: if the container is down, triage still works with Rust-side findings only.
101	
102	## Scope — Documentation Cascade
103	
104	- `apck-claude-context.md`: add `apcrc_container.rs` + `apctc_container.rs` rows to the Source modules table. Update the Detection Architecture section if the integration point warrants a note.
105	- APCPS: minor tightening if any reality diverges from the JSON schema as written; spec is master, Rust types must match it.
106	
107	## Constraints
108	
109	- **No combining logic this pace**. Container findings accumulate in the log, not in the evidence pool yet. Combining is a later pace with empirical input.
110	- **Graceful degradation is non-negotiable**: the app must start and function if the container is not running. Ann should not be blocked by a missing or crashed container.
111	- **Timeout budget**: 30s is a placeholder — adjust based on observed container latency during smoke testing.
112	
113	## Exit Criteria
114	
115	- `tt/apcw-t.Test.sh` passes including new `apctc_container` tests. `deny(warnings)` holds.
116	- `tt/apcw-fl.FixtureLoad.sh` followed by focus, with the container running, produces:
117	  - `$HOME/apcjd/{N}-in.txt` with normalized text
118	  - `$HOME/apcjd/{N}.json` from the container (within 30s)
119	  - `apcap.log` line: `container output received: stanford=S spacy=C stanza=Z`
120	- Same fixture load with the container **stopped** produces a graceful timeout warning in `apcap.log` and normal triage behavior in the UI.
121	- `apck-claude-context.md` source modules table includes `apcrc` / `apctc` entries.
122	
123	## Out of Scope
124	
125	- Combining rules — deferred to a later pace once empirical data from this integration is available.
126	- UI consumption of container findings — container findings are log-only this pace.
127	- Decomposing the existing Rust-side `apcrm_match.rs` into five separate scans to match APCS0 — that's a refactoring pace, orthogonal from this integration, can be deferred until combining is spec'd.
128	- Performance tuning of the container roundtrip.
129	- Anonymized-output (`{N}-out.txt`) awareness of container findings — still derives from Rust-only findings until combining exists.

- **Graceful degradation is non-negotiable**: the app must start and function if the container is not running. Ann should not be blocked by a missing or crashed container.
- **Timeout budget**: 30s is a placeholder — adjust based on observed container latency during smoke testing.

## Exit Criteria

- `tt/apcw-t.Test.sh` passes including new `apctc_container` tests. `deny(warnings)` holds.
- `tt/apcw-fl.FixtureLoad.sh` followed by focus, with the container running, produces:
  - `$HOME/apcjd/{N}-in.txt` with normalized text
  - `$HOME/apcjd/{N}.json` from the container (within 30s)
  - `apcap.log` line: `container output received: stanford=S spacy=C stanza=Z`
- Same fixture load with the container **stopped** produces a graceful timeout warning in `apcap.log` and normal triage behavior in the UI.
- `apck-claude-context.md` source modules table includes `apcrc` / `apctc` entries.

## Out of Scope

- Combining rules — deferred to a later pace once empirical data from this integration is available.
- UI consumption of container findings — container findings are log-only this pace.
- Decomposing the existing Rust-side `apcrm_match.rs` into five separate scans to match APCS0 — that's a refactoring pace, orthogonal from this integration, can be deferred until combining is spec'd.
- Performance tuning of the container roundtrip.
- Anonymized-output (`{N}-out.txt`) awareness of container findings — still derives from Rust-only findings until combining exists.

Mounted. Here's the context.

**Heat:** `apck-1-proof-of-life` (₣A9) — APCK prototype racing to a working loop: clipboard → detection → anonymized output.

**Paddock summary:** Tauri + Rust clipboard tool for Epic EHR → Open Evidence PHI triage. Ten-discerner architecture across two placements (7 Rust in-process, 3 in a bind-mounted container). APCS0 and APCPS are master; combining is deferred until empirical evidence arrives.

**Recent work:** ₢A9AAZ (container-scaffolding) landed with smoke + fixture-assay validation. Container produces schema-compatible `{N}.json` at ~370 KB per page. Rust side has been untouched through the last three paces — this pace is the Rust-side wiring.

**Next pace:** ₢A9AAa `rust-container-integration` — the Rust half of the loop. Three scopes:
1. **Harvest**: write `$HOME/apcjd/{N}-in.txt` (normalized text) on Clinical branch in `apcrh_harvest.rs`, plus `apcth` tests.
2. **Container module**: new `apcrc_container.rs` with serde types mirroring the APCPS JSON schemas (Stanford/spaCy/Stanza with `#[serde(tag = "kind")]` enums) + `apcrc_await_output(journal_dir, index, timeout)` using `notify` crate; new `apctc_container.rs` tests.
3. **Focus handler**: call `apcrc_await_output(... 30s)` after harvest in `zapcap_handle_focus`; log counts on success, warn on timeout. Graceful degradation is load-bearing.

**Plus** documentation cascade to apck-claude-context.md.

**Silks assessment:** `rust-container-integration` fits the docket — no rename needed.

**Proposed approach:**
- Sequential **sonnet** — one file at a time, mostly mechanical plumbing. One judgment beat (timeout budget, graceful-degradation wiring shape) but everything else is spec-driven.
- Order: (1) types + deserialization tests against fixture strings first (proves schema alignment); (2) `apcrc_await_output` with notify + golden-file test; (3) harvest `{N}-in.txt` write + tests; (4) focus handler wire-up; (5) doc cascade; (6) end-to-end smoke with container running and stopped.
- The committed epic fixture JSONs from ₢A9AAZ are convenient test material for (1).

**Risks to flag:**
- `notify` crate isn't currently a dependency — will add it.
- The fixture JSONs are 370 KB; deserializing per focus event is substantial. Spec's "payload pruning candidate" note from the memo may want revisiting, but this pace says no — just deserialize everything.
- 30s timeout interacts with the focus handler — need to confirm it's async/non-blocking so the UI doesn't freeze.

Ready to proceed?

11	**Inputs:** `test_fixtures/epic_initial.txt` (5,147 chars), `test_fixtures/epic_geriatric.txt` (4,988 chars)

14	
15	## Why this memo exists
16	
17	The pace docket's smoke test used a single curated one-liner
18	(`"Patient: Margaret Thornton. DOB: 03/15/1952. Seen at Mercy General by Dr. Chen."`)
19	which exercised the wire contract but not the discerners' behavior on realistic
20	clinical prose. This memo captures the first end-to-end run of both HTML
21	fixtures (after whitespace-normalization via a stdlib HTML-to-text helper that
22	mirrors `zapcrp_normalize_whitespace` in `apcrp_parse.rs`) so that subsequent
23	paces — combining, Rust-side wiring, schema pruning — can reason from evidence
24	rather than assumption.
25	
26	The two JSON files beside this memo are unedited container outputs and should
27	be treated as reference data. They are large because all three discerners emit
28	token-level findings; that size is itself a design data point (see
29	Engineering Notes below).
30	
31	## Headline result
32	
33	The container works end-to-end on realistic clinical text. The APCS0/APCPS
34	complementarity claim — Stanford strong on narrative PHI, weak on structured
35	identifiers; Stanza providing independent corroboration via OntoNotes NER;
36	spaCy contributing syntactic signal — is validated by concrete examples in
37	both outputs.
38	
39	## Stanford de-identifier — strong narrative, weak on structure
40	
41	### What it nailed (confidence ≥ 0.99)
42	
43	- **All patient + family names**: Margaret J. Thornton, Robert Thornton, Ms.
44	  Thornton, Harold W. Eriksen, Karen Eriksen-Moody, Thomas Eriksen, Miriam
45	  Eriksen, Mr. Eriksen.
46	- **All clinicians as HCW**: Dr. Susan Chen, James R. Whitfield MD, Priya
47	  Anand MD, Elena Vasquez PA-C, Naomi Okwu MD, Patricia Nowak, Dr. Anand,
48	  Dr. Whitfield.
49	- **Every date at confidence 1.0**: DOBs, visit dates, historical years —
50	  9 dates on the geriatric fixture including freestanding years (`2024`,
51	  `1961`) and month-year stubs (`04/2026`, `01/2026`).
52	- **All structured IDs that didn't fragment**: MRN `00847293`, account
53	  `3391057842`, SSN `471-83-2956`, BCBS plan `BCBS-MEM-992817445`, device
54	  serial `1234567890`, DEA-style `AW1234567`.
55	- **All phone numbers** across both fixtures.
56	
57	### Where it got confused
58	
59	- **Address ↔ facility conflation.** "1847 Cranberry Lane, Westbrook, ME
60	  04092" (home address) and "Maine Medical Center" (a real hospital) both
61	  tagged `HOSPITAL`. The model has no separate ADDRESS label — APCS0
62	  documents this and the fixture assay makes it concrete.
63	- **ID fragmentation.** `ENC-2026-0051203` on the geriatric fixture split
64	  into two findings: `ENC-` at confidence 0.98 and `-0051203` at 1.0, as
65	  separate `ID` entries. Exactly the "structured identifiers fragment
66	  across labels" failure mode described in APCS0. Combining should prefer
67	  regex evidence for structured IDs.
68	- **URL/email surgery.** Email `m.thornton47@gmail.com` fragmented into
69	  `m.thornton47` tagged PATIENT + `gmail`/`com` tagged VENDOR. Portal URL
70	  `https://mychart.mainehealth.org/patient/thornton-m` fragmented into
71	  `mychart`/`mainehealth` (VENDOR) + `thornton-m` (PATIENT). Regex for
72	  email and URL shapes handles these cleanly.
73	- **Low-confidence noise.** "SS" (0.62) tagged HOSPITAL — a stray fragment
74	  from "SSN" context. Combining can reasonably filter sub-0.7 non-name
75	  labels; every high-signal finding was ≥ 0.95.
76	
77	## spaCy — syntactic gold, NER underwhelming
78	
79	- **1,008 / 942 token findings** (POS, morph, lemma, dep, head). This is
80	  the real value of spaCy in this pipeline: feedstock for homograph
81	  disambiguation in combining (e.g., "May" as proper noun vs. modal verb).
82	- **283 / 290 entity findings, all labeled generically `ENTITY`.**
83	  `en_core_sci_md`'s default NER emits a single entity type — it was
84	  designed for biomedical entity detection, not typed classification. The
85	  NER output is therefore low-value for PHI discrimination; combining
86	  should weight spaCy by its syntactic outputs, not its entity list.
87	
88	## Stanza — complementary NER taxonomy, some leakage
89	
90	- **PERSON captures mirror Stanford exactly**: independent corroboration
91	  on every person name in both fixtures. This is the two-parser
92	  redundancy the spec calls for.
93	- **GPE** (geo-political): Westbrook, Portland, Scarborough, Cape
94	  Elizabeth, South Portland — the locality signal Stanford conflates into
95	  HOSPITAL. Combining can separate "this is a city" (GPE) from "this is
96	  a hospital" (HOSPITAL) using Stanza evidence.
97	- **FAC** (facility): addresses + Maine Medical Center, separating
98	  facility from narrative context more cleanly than Stanford's HOSPITAL
99	  bucket.
100	- **ORG**: mostly right (Maine Medical Center) but with false positives
101	  on medical abbreviations (NSTEMI, PCI, ED, EMS) — Stanza treats
102	  all-caps strings as organizations.
103	- **CARDINAL leakage**: `471-83-2956` (SSN) and `555-0143` (phone
104	  fragment) tagged `CARDINAL` — PHI hidden as "just a number".
105	  `207-555-0488` tagged `TIME`. Combining should recognize that
106	  structured-ID regex evidence dominates Stanza's numeric
107	  misclassifications.
108	
109	## Predictions from APCS0 / APCPS — validated
110	
111	1. **Stanford narrative strength + structured weakness** — confirmed with
112	   concrete examples.
113	2. **Two independent parsers corroborating PERSON** — confirmed.
114	   Stanford PATIENT/HCW and Stanza PERSON agree across every name in
115	   both documents.
116	3. **NER taxonomy complementarity** — confirmed. Stanza GPE, FAC,
117	   CARDINAL give combining signals Stanford lacks.
118	4. **Regex will earn its keep** — confirmed. SSN, phone, encounter ID,
119	   email, URL all parse cleaner in regex than any of the three models.
120	
121	## Engineering notes worth carrying forward
122	
123	- **JSON size: ~370 KB per normalized-page.** For the Rust consumer,
124	  this is substantial deserialization per clipboard event. Options to
125	  consider in the next pace: (a) schema-side prune in the container
126	  (e.g., drop token-level findings whose `pos` is punctuation,
127	  drop Stanza CARDINAL when the surface matches a known regex
128	  structured-ID shape); (b) Rust-side streaming parse that skips
129	  uninteresting records.
130	- **Steady-state throughput is fast.** Both fixtures processed in
131	  under a second each after warmup. The docket's 30–90 s estimate
132	  referred to cold model load; once `ready`, discerner latency per
133	  document is negligible at this text size.
134	- **Drop-old staging matters for testing.** The container processes
135	  only the highest-indexed `{N}-in.txt`. If you copy both fixtures
136	  into `~/apcjd/` before the scanner tick, only the higher-numbered
137	  file gets processed. The assay sequenced 10001 → wait for 10001.json
138	  → 10002 → wait for 10002.json. Worth calling out in the container
139	  README.
140	- **Container security posture held.** Runtime was `--network=none
141	  --cap-drop=all --read-only --user nobody:nogroup` throughout both
142	  assays. No network-exfiltration surface; PHI stayed within the
143	  bind-mount.
144	
145	## Items for subsequent paces to study
146	
147	These are flagged by the data, not prescribed. The design conversation
148	can continue from here.
149	
150	- **Combining rule for HOSPITAL vs. ADDRESS vs. GPE**: Stanford's
151	  HOSPITAL label is doing triple duty. A combining rule that triangulates
152	  Stanford HOSPITAL + Stanza FAC/GPE + regex address shape into distinct
153	  apcs_phi_category values would eliminate the conflation.

162	- **JSON payload pruning**: if the Rust consumer only needs tokens for
163	  specific homograph disambiguation cases, token-level findings could
164	  be filtered in the container to reduce JSON size by an order of

166	  token-level features broadly or only selectively.
167	- **VENDOR label as PHI**: `gmail`, `com`, `mychart`, `mainehealth` are
168	  all technically low-entropy strings caught by the VENDOR label.
169	  Whether Safe Harbor requires these scrubbed is an APCAS question,
170	  not a detection-pipeline question — but the evidence is collected
171	  either way.
172	
173	## Reproduction

177	python3 Tools/apck/.../normalize.py  # ad hoc during this run
178	
179	# Container lifecycle
180	tt/apcw-cb.ContainerBuild.sh
181	tt/apcw-cs.ContainerStart.sh

content.text:
Here's the reconsideration. Three categories: things the empirical data invalidates, things it nuances, things it adds.

## Invalidates

**1. `apcrc_await_output(journal_dir, index, Duration)` is wrong for `{index}.json`.**
The container's drop-old policy means: if the user focuses twice in rapid succession, the second focus writes `{N+1}-in.txt`, the container's next tick picks the highest, and `{N}.json` **is never produced**. The proposed signature would time out falsely on a working container. The contract must be "wait for any `{M}.json` with M ≥ N" and, if M > N, log that our index was dropped. This is a real semantic change, not a tuning knob.

**2. 30s timeout is wildly over-provisioned.**
Memo: steady-state sub-1s per fixture. The 30s came from the original 30-90s cold-load estimate in the scaffolding docket, which empirically is ~8s on first `ready`, not per-event. If we assume container is ready before Ann focuses (or we log readiness separately on app start), per-event timeout is closer to 3-5s. The 30s isn't broken but it's a lie about what we expect.

## Nuances

**3. The `ApcrcSpacyFinding::Entity` variant is structurally required, semantically zero.**
Every spaCy entity is labeled generic `ENTITY` (en_core_sci_md's NER limitation). The wire format still contains them, so Rust types must accept them, but every future combining pass will weight them at zero. We should still deserialize (contract = contract), but the log line should call out the asymmetry so we don't later waste eyes squinting at "283 spacy entities".

**4. Payload is ~370 KB per focus event.**
Deserializing this per clipboard focus on an M1+ Mac is fine (milliseconds), but we should measure and record it in the first real run so we have numbers for when combining lands. Not a blocker, but warrants a one-line benchmark in the test module (parse the committed fixture, assert under a generous wall-clock ceiling, record actual).

**5. Decompose the log line.**
Docket prescribes `stanford=S spacy=C stanza=Z`. Given the memo's findings (spaCy C is ~90% syntactic tokens + useless entities; Stanza Z is same), a more honest line:
```
container output received for N (took_from=M): stanford=39 spacy_tok=1008 spacy_ent=283 stanza_tok=942 stanza_ent=195
```
`took_from=M` makes drop-old behavior visible when it happens.

## Adds

**6. Rapid-focus handling is now a real question.**
The docket treats the loop as a single synchronous write → await → log. With drop-old in play, the focus handler needs a concurrency discipline: spawn the await on a detached task (so UI isn't blocked), and tolerate superseded awaits gracefully. The simplest shape: await returns either `Output(M, payload)` or `SupersededOrTimeout(err)`. Focus handler logs, never blocks UI, never races.

**7. APCPS needs a small edit.**
Either the spec should explicitly document the "wait for M ≥ N" semantics on the consumer side, or the container should emit a "dropped N" log line that Rust can correlate. The current spec is silent and the memo's "drop-old staging requires sequential copy-then-wait" sentence is only in the scaffolding notes. Spec is master — let's write it down.

## Out-of-scope stays out

Memo flags payload pruning, schema simplification, and spaCy entity elimination as future work. I agree we don't touch the wire this pace. Combining still deferred.

## Proposed revised scope

1. **Harvest** — unchanged from docket (write `{N}-in.txt`).
2. **Container module** —
   - Types as specified (match wire exactly).
   - `apcrc_await_output(journal_dir, from_index, timeout) -> Result<ApcrcContainerOutput>` returns the **highest-M `{M}.json` where M ≥ from_index**, not specifically `{from_index}.json`.
   - Return type includes the actual M so focus handler can log `took_from=M`.
3. **Focus handler** —
   - Spawn the await on a non-blocking task.
   - 5s timeout (tuned from memo), not 30s.
   - Decomposed log line with per-kind counts + `took_from`.
4. **APCPS edit** — document consumer-side "wait for M ≥ N" semantics and the drop-old implications.
5. **Test** — one fixture test that parses the committed `epic_initial.json` from the memo directory and asserts structural shape + rough counts. No golden equality (wire values will drift), but a sanity check that the serde layout matches reality.

**Cut from docket:** none. Just reshaping the container-module contract and the focus-handler discipline to match what the container actually does.

Does this match your read? Specifically want your call on:
- The M ≥ N waiting semantics (significant departure from the docket wording but, I think, forced by empirical behavior)
- 5s vs 30s timeout
- Whether APCPS edits are in-scope for this pace or deferred

## Paces

### rust-container-integration (₢A9AAa) [rough]

## Character

Rust code wiring the existing Clipbuddy focus handler to the container's bind-mount interface. Not architectural — APCS0 and APCPS already pin every contract this pace consumes. Sonnet-scale, mostly mechanical plumbing with one interesting beat (graceful degradation when the container isn't up).

## Prerequisites

₢A9AAZ (`container-scaffolding`) must land first — the container has to exist and produce valid `{N}.json` before Rust can integrate with it. Smoke-test validation of the container is the entry gate for this pace.

## Context

A9AAV decided: Rust Clipbuddy stays the frontend; sophisticated algorithms live in the container; combining stays in Rust (strongly typed). ₢A9AAZ constructs the container in isolation. This pace builds the Rust half: write `{N}-in.txt`, watch for `{N}.json`, deserialize, log. No combining yet — this pace proves the loop, the next pace (combining-v1, deferred) does the intelligence once empirical evidence is in hand.

## Scope — Harvest Update

Update `apcrh_harvest.rs`:

- On the Clinical branch, after the existing raw-flavor captures (`{N}-in.clinical.{ext}` family), also write `$HOME/apcjd/{N}-in.txt` containing `apcs_normalized_text`: plain UTF-8 output of the existing HTML-strip / whitespace-collapse / encoding-normalization path.
- The bare `{N}-in.txt` name (no `{tag}` infix, no UTI suffix) is documented in APCPS Clipboard Harvest section as the container's input file.
- Non-clinical branch still writes only raw captures; no `{N}-in.txt` on that branch (no detection pipeline runs).
- Write failure is non-fatal: log via `apcrl_error_now!` and continue. Matches the existing harvest failure-mode discipline.

Update `apcth_harvest.rs`:

- Add tests covering `{N}-in.txt` write on Clinical branch.
- Confirm the scanner's leading-digit parse still handles the expanded file family correctly.

## Scope — Container Integration Module

Create `apcd/src/apcrc_container.rs`:

- Rust types mirroring the APCPS JSON schemas:
  - `ApcrcStanfordFinding { text, start, end, label, confidence }`
  - `ApcrcSpacyFinding` — enum over `Token { text, start, end, pos, tag, morph, lemma, head, dep }` and `Entity { text, start, end, label }` variants discriminated by the `kind` field.
  - `ApcrcStanzaFinding` — same pattern, `Token { text, start, end, upos, xpos, feats, lemma, head, deprel, ner }` and `Entity { text, start, end, label }`.
  - `ApcrcContainerOutput { index, stanford: { findings: Vec<Stanford> }, spacy: { findings: Vec<Spacy> }, stanza: { findings: Vec<Stanza> } }` at top level.
- `serde::Deserialize` derives on all types. Use `#[serde(tag = "kind")]` for the token/entity enums.
- Function `apcrc_await_output(journal_dir: &Path, index: u64, timeout: Duration) -> Result<ApcrcContainerOutput>` that uses the `notify` crate (FSEvents on macOS) to watch for `{index}.json` existing, then reads and deserializes. Returns an informative error on timeout or on deserialize failure.

Create `apcd/src/apctc_container.rs`:

- Tests against fixture JSON strings matching the APCPS schema for each discerner variant — token + entity, each with representative field values. Confirm deserialization produces the expected Rust values.
- Add a golden-file test: a small fixture `{N}.json` on disk, `apcrc_await_output` picks it up and deserializes correctly.

Wire into `lib.rs`:

- `pub mod apcrc_container;`
- `#[cfg(test)] mod apctc_container;`

## Scope — Focus Handler Wire-up

Update `apcap_main.rs::zapcap_handle_focus`:

- On Clinical branch, after the existing harvest + anonymized-output write path, call `apcrc_await_output(journal_dir, N, Duration::from_secs(30))`.
- On success: log `[INFO] container output received: stanford={S} spacy={C} stanza={Z}` where the three counts are the `findings.len()` from each discerner key. No combining, no UI consumption — just the log line confirming the loop works.
- On timeout or error: log `[WARN] container output not received within 30s for index N: {error}`. Focus handler continues normally — existing Rust-only triage path is authoritative for UI.
- The graceful-degradation discipline is load-bearing: if the container is down, triage still works with Rust-side findings only.

## Scope — Documentation Cascade

- `apck-claude-context.md`: add `apcrc_container.rs` + `apctc_container.rs` rows to the Source modules table. Update the Detection Architecture section if the integration point warrants a note.
- APCPS: minor tightening if any reality diverges from the JSON schema as written; spec is master, Rust types must match it.

## Constraints

- **No combining logic this pace**. Container findings accumulate in the log, not in the evidence pool yet. Combining is a later pace with empirical input.
- **Graceful degradation is non-negotiable**: the app must start and function if the container is not running. Ann should not be blocked by a missing or crashed container.
- **Timeout budget**: 30s is a placeholder — adjust based on observed container latency during smoke testing.

## Exit Criteria

- `tt/apcw-t.Test.sh` passes including new `apctc_container` tests. `deny(warnings)` holds.
- `tt/apcw-fl.FixtureLoad.sh` followed by focus, with the container running, produces:
  - `$HOME/apcjd/{N}-in.txt` with normalized text
  - `$HOME/apcjd/{N}.json` from the container (within 30s)
  - `apcap.log` line: `container output received: stanford=S spacy=C stanza=Z`
- Same fixture load with the container **stopped** produces a graceful timeout warning in `apcap.log` and normal triage behavior in the UI.
- `apck-claude-context.md` source modules table includes `apcrc` / `apctc` entries.

## Out of Scope

- Combining rules — deferred to a later pace once empirical data from this integration is available.
- UI consumption of container findings — container findings are log-only this pace.
- Decomposing the existing Rust-side `apcrm_match.rs` into five separate scans to match APCS0 — that's a refactoring pace, orthogonal from this integration, can be deferred until combining is spec'd.
- Performance tuning of the container roundtrip.
- Anonymized-output (`{N}-out.txt`) awareness of container findings — still derives from Rust-only findings until combining exists.

### capture-declared-pasteboard-flavors (₢A9AAY) [rough]

## Character

Mechanical capture work on top of one genuine unknown (cocoa/objc2 FFI shape for NSPasteboard enumeration). First tranche is a small spike — figure out the right call pattern for `NSPasteboard.generalPasteboard()`, item enumeration, declared-types list, and per-type `dataForType:` bytes. The rest is plumbing that matches the classification-tag pattern already laid down in `apcrh_harvest.rs`. The detector is **not touched** — it still reads UTF-8 via arboard. This pace is strictly "put every declared flavor on disk so the evaluation pace has real inputs."

## Problem

Ann's empirical `osascript -e 'clipboard info'` showed five declared UTIs after an Epic Copy All: `public.rtf` (139559 bytes), `public.utf16-plain-text` (52834), `public.utf8-plain-text` (26461 — exactly what the classifier saw), `Unicode text` (52832, legacy UTF-16 alias), and a 52-byte `string`. **No `public.html`** — contradicting the ₢A9AAW docket's premise. Word pastes formatted because it reads `public.rtf`.

Current harvest, layered on arboard, sees at most two flavors: `get_text()` (UTF-8 plain) and `get().html()` (which honestly returns `ContentNotAvailable` against a board that has no HTML). Arboard has no public API for RTF, nor for enumerating declared UTIs. To get the full picture on disk we have to go one layer lower.

Without this pace, the evaluation pace that follows has nothing to evaluate against — only the UTF-8 flavor that already failed once.

## Solution

Use cocoa/objc2 FFI directly against `NSPasteboard` to (a) enumerate the **declared** types of the first pasteboard item and (b) fetch each type's bytes via `dataForType:`. Write each flavor to the journal directory alongside the existing capture pattern, preserving the Clinical/NonClinical tag from `apcrh_Classification`. Only *declared* types are captured — not macOS-synthesized ones (those can balloon to 15+ per item; what the producer actually wrote is what we want to study). This matches the behavior of the `osascript 'clipboard info'` diagnostic.

Filename shape: `{N}-in.{tag}.{ext}` where `{ext}` is derived from the UTI via a small mapping table. Unknown UTIs fall through to a sanitized `.{com-example-thing}.bin` form so the flavor space is extensible without code changes.

## Deliverables — Code

- **New module** `apcrk_pasteboard.rs` (prefix `apcrk` — K for kernel/pasteboard, following the source-module convention). Single public entry: `apcrk_capture_declared_flavors(dir, classification, index) -> Result<Vec<String>, String>` returning the list of filenames written. Internals: `unsafe` cocoa/objc2 calls for `generalPasteboard`, `pasteboardItems`, `types`, `dataForType`. FFI surface kept tight and type-safe at the boundary.
- **UTI-to-extension mapper** — data-driven table. Known mappings:
  - `public.rtf` → `rtf`
  - `public.utf8-plain-text` → `utf8.txt`
  - `public.utf16-plain-text` → `utf16.txt`
  - `public.html` → `html` (defensive — if it ever appears)
  - `public.tiff`, `public.png` → corresponding extensions
  - legacy NSPasteboard names (`NSStringPboardType`, `Unicode text`) → sanitized passthrough
  - anything else → sanitized-uti with `.bin` suffix (dots → hyphens, forbidden filename chars scrubbed)
- **`apcrh_harvest::apcrh_capture_all_flavors`**: delegate to the new module. The existing hand-coded arboard `get_text` / `get().html()` writers become a fallback *only* if the new path returns zero flavors (defensive — should never happen in practice).
- **`apcap_main::zapcap_handle_focus`**: no changes beyond the existing harvest call site. Classifier still reads UTF-8 via arboard's `get_text()`.
- **Cargo.toml**: add `objc2` and any `objc2-app-kit` / `objc2-foundation` subcrates needed. Pin versions.
- **Tests** (`apctk_pasteboard.rs`):
  - UTI-to-extension mapping, known and unknown UTIs.
  - Sanitization of forbidden filename characters in unknown UTIs.
  - FFI paths cannot be unit-tested without a real NSPasteboard; verification is via deployed run on Ann's machine.

## Deliverables — Spec (APCPS)

- **Clipboard Harvest → Flavors table**: replace the current 2-row (`text`/`html`) table with a description of declared-flavor enumeration. Note that the table becomes illustrative — actual flavors captured depend on what the producer (Epic, browser, any app) wrote.
- **Clipboard Harvest → Naming paragraph**: extend extension description to include UTI-derived mapping and the `.{sanitized-uti}.bin` fallback.
- **Clipboard Harvest → Scope note**: explicit statement that only declared types are captured, not macOS-synthesized ones. Reference the `osascript 'clipboard info'` diagnostic as the parity target.
- **Short note in the Flavors section**: arboard remains on the classifier's `get_text()` path; the new FFI module owns enumeration + multi-flavor capture. Separation of concerns is load-bearing — arboard is simple and stable, the FFI module is lower-level and riskier.

## Constraints

- **Declared types only** — never capture macOS-synthesized derivatives. Match `osascript 'clipboard info'` output.
- **arboard stays on the classifier path.** No regression to `get_text()`-based detection. The FFI module writes to disk; the classifier reads through arboard.
- **FFI failures are non-fatal.** A broken NSPasteboard call logs via `apcrl_error_now!` and lets harvest fall back to the arboard path. Triage continues regardless.
- **Tag classification preserved across all flavors** — `10001-in.clinical.rtf`, `10001-in.clinical.utf8.txt`, etc. The enum decided at the start of the focus handler applies uniformly.
- **Index space unified.** Scanner in `apcrh_harvest` keeps the leading-digit-run parse — every new extension counts toward the same index space.
- **Unsafe is isolated.** All FFI lives in `apcrk_pasteboard.rs`. No `unsafe` bleeds into `apcrh_harvest` or `apcap_main`.

## Exit Criteria

- `tt/apcw-t.Test.sh` passes, including new UTI mapping tests. `deny(warnings)` holds.
- Focus on Epic "Copy All" produces on disk: `{N}-in.clinical.rtf`, `{N}-in.clinical.utf16.txt`, `{N}-in.clinical.utf8.txt`, and any other declared UTIs present. **RTF must be present with nonzero size** — primary acceptance signal.
- Focus on a plain-text non-clinical clipboard still produces at least `{N}-in.nonclinical.utf8.txt`.
- Unknown UTIs (if Epic or some other source writes them) land as `.{sanitized}.bin` rather than failing the capture.
- APCPS Clipboard Harvest section updated: Flavors table reshaped, Naming paragraph extended, scope note on declared-only added.
- Deployed to Ann's machine; next Copy All verified to produce RTF + UTF-16 + UTF-8 files in `~/apcjd/`.

## Out of Scope

- **Interpreting any captured flavor.** Reading RTF, converting RTF→HTML, enriching text parsing — all belong to the evaluation pace (₢A9AAX) and beyond.
- **Changing the classifier's entry point** from `get_text()` to anything richer. That's a downstream implementation pace.
- **Selecting a permanent RTF parser** as a Cargo dep. The evaluation pace decides; this pace does not.
- **Synthesized UTIs.** Only declared ones.
- **Image flavor interpretation.** `.tiff` / `.png` bytes land on disk; they are not decoded, thumbnailed, or forwarded.
- **Cross-platform parity.** This is Mac-first; Windows pasteboard enumeration is a future pace.

### evaluate-detection-across-flavors (₢A9AAX) [rough]

## Character

Exploratory research, not implementation. This pace produces a **docket**, not shipped code. Brad runs the existing detector against the flavors captured by the preceding pace, observes where it succeeds and fails, and emerges with a defended choice: "classify from X by method Y." Cognitive posture is empirical/comparative — resist the urge to implement a winning path inside this pace; the conclusion is input to the *next* pace.

## Problem

After the preceding pace lands, the journal directory holds per-flavor captures for every Epic Copy All. The classifier currently reads only UTF-8 plain text (`clipboard.get_text()` via arboard), and that path classified a 26461-byte Epic copy as non-clinical because the clinical heuristic depends on structural cues (labels, section headers) that the UTF-8 flavor strips. RTF carries vastly more structure — 139559 bytes vs 26461 on Ann's captured session — but isn't HTML, and our existing parser (`apcrp_parse.rs`) assumes HTML.

Three plausible paths exist, each with different integration cost and different failure modes:
1. **Classify from RTF directly** — add an RTF parser, extract labels/sections, feed Tier 2/3.
2. **RTF → HTML pre-pass** — convert, keep `apcrp_parse.rs` unchanged. Lowest structural risk, but adds conversion artifacts.
3. **Enrich the UTF-8 text pipeline** — strengthen Tier 2 regex anchors, accept that some Epic structure is lost. Cheapest.

Without running the detector against real captures, we can't tell which path's tradeoffs are acceptable. Committing to one by argument alone repeats the mistake of the ₢A9AAW premise.

## Solution

Hands-on comparison using real captures from Ann's journal. Brad writes a throwaway `apcab`-style scratch binary that takes a flavor file + format hint and runs the detector, emitting a comparable finding report. Runs the detector against RTF, UTF-8, and (if applicable) UTF-16 from the same capture. Judges:

- **Recall** — which flavor's findings best match the known PHI in that capture?
- **Precision** — does any flavor introduce new false positives?
- **Integration cost** — what does it take to actually wire the winning flavor into the main pipeline?

Writes a comparison report and a successor pace docket with concrete deliverables.

## Deliverables

- Throwaway flavor-evaluation binary. May live outside the main tree (`apck/apcd/src/apcxx_scratch.rs` or similar, excluded from `cargo test`). Reads a captured file, asks the detector for findings, writes a report to stdout or a text file.
- Comparison notes — brief, one paragraph per candidate path (RTF-direct / RTF→HTML / enrich-UTF-8). Must include a rejection rationale for the unchosen paths, not just a selection.
- A new pace docket describing the chosen path with deliverables, constraints, exit criteria. Slated into this heat, ready for execution.

## Constraints

- **Real captures only.** Synthetic fixtures don't tell us what actually-delivered RTF looks like. At least one real Epic Copy All with known PHI — Ann clicks through a test encounter in her sandbox, Brad reviews.
- **Evaluation binary is disposable.** No tests, no polish, no commit churn. Output is the docket, not the binary.
- **Rejection rationales are load-bearing.** "We picked X because Y, and rejected Z because W" — not just "we picked X." Future you will thank present you.
- **No detector changes in this pace.** Tuning dictionaries, regex, or severity merging is orthogonal and belongs elsewhere.

## Exit Criteria

- Two or more real Epic captures processed through the evaluation binary; per-flavor finding reports on disk.
- Comparison notes written, arguing for the winning path.
- New implementation pace slated into this heat with a full docket ready for mount.
- Evaluation scratch code archived, deleted, or clearly marked disposable.

## Out of Scope

- Implementing the chosen detection path. That's the docket-that-emerges-from-this-pace's job.
- Shipping the evaluation binary. It's scaffolding.
- Classifier tuning (dictionaries, regex, severity merging) — orthogonal.
- RTF parser selection as a permanent Cargo dep — if the winning path is RTF-direct, dep selection is part of the implementation pace, not this one.

### deploy-and-real-data-test (₢A9AAL) [rough]

## Character

Field enablement. Ship a harvest-capable build to Ann, confirm captures accumulate on first real Epic use, and establish the feedback cadence. This is the trigger that starts fixture collection — not a validation milestone for the current detection engine.

## Prerequisites

- `epic-capture-harvest` (₢A9AAR) complete; harvest mechanism live in `apcap_main.rs` and APCPS `Clipboard Harvest` section merged.
- `tt/apcw-b.Build.sh` clean on developer machine.

## Deploy

1. Build release: `tt/apcw-b.Build.sh`.
2. Deploy: `tt/apcw-d.Deploy.sh` (scp to `anns-macbook-air:/Users/Shared/apcua/`).
3. Self-update watcher picks up the new bundle on next focus; manual launch if app not running.

## First Real Capture Session

1. On Ann's machine, open Epic, navigate to a patient chart.
2. Epic "Copy All" to copy a clinical note.
3. Switch to PHI Clipbuddy — triage view appears.
4. Confirm by listing: `ls -la $HOME/apck_harvest/` — expect at least one `10000.{html,txt}` pair (and any additional flavors the clipboard abstraction surfaced).
5. Process a second note — expect `10001.*` to appear.

## What to Observe (not validate)

- Flavor inventory: which extensions actually land? HTML always? Plain text always? RTF ever? Image ever?
- HTML shape: is Epic's HTML similar to the synthetic fixtures, or structurally different?
- Detection quality: RED/YELLOW categorization on real content — note misses and overcatches, but do NOT fix inline. Capture observations for the next iteration.

## Cadence Establishment

- Ann continues to use the app during normal Epic workflow.
- Captures accumulate in `$HOME/apck_harvest/` — outside any git tree, manually reviewed.
- User (developer) coordinates anonymization and fixture promotion out-of-band.

## Exit Criteria

- At least two real Epic captures written to `$HOME/apck_harvest/` on Ann's machine with both HTML and text flavors present.
- Flavor inventory noted (which extensions appeared).
- First observations about Epic's real HTML shape recorded — paddock discussion or a follow-up itch is acceptable.
- Ann comfortable with the capture workflow; no further deploy needed for routine use.

## Out of Scope

- Detection quality tuning (next iteration, once anonymized fixtures exist).
- Anonymization procedure definition (user coordinates manually).
- Open Evidence paste validation (may happen, but not the gating deliverable).
- False-positive / false-negative scoring (needs anonymized fixtures first).

### fixture-walkthrough-debug (₢A9AAK) [rough]

## Character

Guided manual verification. No code changes expected — this is a structured test session using the two synthetic fixtures to confirm the full pipeline works end-to-end on the developer's machine. Debugging character: if something fails, diagnose and fix inline.

## Prerequisites

- App builds clean: `tt/apcw-b.Build.sh` or `cargo build` in apcd/
- Two fixture loaders available: `tt/apcw-fl.FixtureLoad.progress.sh` and `tt/apcw-fl.FixtureLoad.geriatric.sh`

## Walkthrough — Progress Note Fixture

1. Run `tt/apcw-r.Run.sh` to launch the app
2. Run `tt/apcw-fl.FixtureLoad.progress.sh` to load progress note HTML onto clipboard
3. Switch to the app window — triage view should appear
4. Confirm: RED findings include Patient (Margaret Thornton), Attending (Dr. Sarah Whitfield), DOB date, SSN, MRN, phone, email, address, URL
5. Confirm: YELLOW findings include dictionary-matched names in narrative
6. Confirm: DOB shows the date highlighted, not an age yet (age appears in anonymized output)
7. Toggle two YELLOW items to PASS — confirm preview updates (grey styling)
8. Toggle one RED item to PASS — confirm preview updates
9. Click "Copy Anonymized to Clipboard" — button should show "Copied!"
10. Paste into a text editor. Verify:
    - Elided items show category placeholders: `[NAME]`, `[SSN]`, `[PHONE]`, `[EMAIL]`, `[ADDRESS]`, etc.
    - DOB shows `Age: 74` (or appropriate computed age)
    - Items toggled to PASS show original text
    - Line breaks and section structure preserved — readable, not a wall
    - No PHI leaks in elided regions

## Walkthrough — Geriatric Consult Fixture

1. Run `tt/apcw-fl.FixtureLoad.geriatric.sh` to load geriatric HTML onto clipboard
2. Switch to app — triage view should refresh with new content
3. Confirm: clinical content detected, findings populated
4. Click "Copy Anonymized to Clipboard" with all defaults (everything elided)
5. Paste and verify: all PHI replaced, structure preserved
6. If geriatric fixture has a DOB for patient >89 years old, confirm `Age: 90+` appears

## Edge Cases to Probe

- Refocus without changing clipboard — should show "unchanged", triage preserved
- Copy non-clinical text (e.g., "hello world") — should show non-clinical diagnostic, triage cleared
- After non-clinical, click "Copy Anonymized" — should show error (no clinical data)
- Refocus with original fixture again — full triage should reappear

## Exit Criteria

All walkthrough steps pass on both fixtures. Any bugs found are fixed and notched inline before wrapping.

### ssa-download-resolution (₢A9AAM) [rough]

## Character

Interactive design decision. The premise is clear and the options are enumerated — this pace is a conversation to pick a path, then a small implementation.

## Problem

The `apcad` dictionary refresh binary cannot download SSA Baby Names data programmatically. SSA uses Akamai bot protection that 403s non-browser HTTP clients (ureq, curl). The current workaround requires a human to download `names.zip` in a browser and place it at a staging path (`.ssa_names_staging.zip`). This builds a human into a loop that should be automatable.

## Options Identified

1. **Commit the staging zip** (~7.7MB, public domain). Tool already prefers staging file over download. Eliminates human-in-loop entirely. Update by committing a new zip whenever new year data matters.
2. **Use an alternative mirror** (GitHub, Kaggle, data.gov). Avoids Akamai but trades URL stability.
3. **Browser-mimicking HTTP** (curl-impersonate, crafted TLS). Arms race with Akamai.
4. **Headless browser** (chromiumoxide, playwright). Heavy dependency for one file.
5. **Accept the status quo** — staging zip is a one-time manual step that persists indefinitely. SSA data is historical; new year files are annual.

## Deliverables

- Interactive decision on which option to pursue
- Implement the chosen resolution
- Remove or update the staging fallback messaging if no longer needed

### entity-propagation-tier (₢A9AAN) [rough]

## Character

Focused detection enhancement. Mechanical implementation following established Tier 2/3 patterns — no design ambiguity. The aho-corasick infrastructure already exists for Tier 3; this reuses it with a dynamic phrase dictionary built per-document.

## Problem

Tier 2 (label-anchored) identifies entities like "Maine Medical Center" from the `Facility:` field, but when the same phrase appears in narrative text, no tier catches it. Tier 3 (dictionary) operates on individual words, and common words like "Medical" and "Center" are whitelisted. The identifying unit is the multi-word phrase, not the individual tokens.

## Approach

Add entity propagation between Tier 2 and Tier 3 in `apcre_engine.rs`:

1. After `apcrm_scan_anchored` completes, collect the full multi-word values from labeled field spans (Facility, Patient, Attending, Provider, Referring, etc.)
2. Build a case-insensitive aho-corasick automaton from those phrases
3. Scan `plain_text` for matches — emit RED findings with a new `Propagated` mechanism variant
4. Existing merge logic (`zapcre_merge_findings`) handles overlap with Tier 2's original findings

**Whitespace normalization**: Already handled by the parser (`zapcrp_normalize_whitespace`) — both `plain_text` and span values are single-space-normalized. No additional normalization needed.

**Category**: Inherit the category from the originating Tier 2 finding (Facility stays Facility, Provider stays Provider, etc.)

## Scope

- Modify `apcrm_match.rs`: add `Propagated` to `apcrm_Mechanism` enum
- Modify `apcre_engine.rs`: add propagation scan between Tier 2 and Tier 3 calls
- Add unit tests for propagation (facility in narrative, provider in signature block)
- No frontend changes needed — findings render the same regardless of mechanism

### apcs-combining-algorithm (₢A9AAV) [rough]

## Character

Open design conversation requiring judgment. The question "how do multiple discerners' outputs get fused into a single unified classification?" has no mechanical answer — it requires choosing between competing shapes (role/disposition terminology, rule formulation, dictionary asymmetry handling, clinician-toggle composition). Opus-scale: several non-obvious decisions, each with downstream consequences through the spec and eventual code.

## Prerequisites

Pace ₢A9AAU (`apcs-discerner-restructure`) must land first. That pace installs the architectural skeleton (parallel discerners, normalized-text input, anonymization output) and stubs the reconciliation section. This pace fills the stub.

## Context

In the same session that produced ₢A9AAU, a second design conversation began but was deliberately deferred rather than rushed. The open questions are known and named; the answers are not.

## Open Questions Carried Forward

1. **Role / disposition terminology — what replaces severity?**
   - Candidate A: unified classification carries a role from `{TEXT, PHI, DECIDE}` per segment
   - Candidate B: findings carry a disposition from `{cover, surface}` individually
   - Candidate C: a third shape not yet surfaced
   - The choice has downstream effects on anonymization, triage UI, and every discerner output schema
2. **Dictionary internal disposition.** Does `apcs_dictionary_finding` carry a self-dispositioned field (confident vs. ambiguous, derived from whitelist corroboration), or does reconciliation derive this from external rules? Earlier conversation leaned toward internal — the dictionary has the whitelist, so it's the one with the evidence — but the asymmetry with other discerners needs to be documented honestly.
3. **Clinician toggle composition.** Three candidate shapes:
   - Clinician as a discerner producing `apcs_clinician_finding` that enters reconciliation
   - Clinician as a post-reconciliation mutator operating on `apcs_unified_classification`
   - Some third shape (e.g., clinician-as-disposition-override)
4. **Granularity reconciliation.** When two discerners produce findings at different granularities over overlapping text (e.g., `apcs_stanford_finding` covering `Margaret J. Thornton` and `apcs_dictionary_finding` covering just `Margaret`), how is the unified classification sized? Phrase wins? Union? Explicit rule per discerner pair?

## Scope

- Resolve each open question above with concrete design decisions
- Write the concrete reconciliation rules — pairwise as needed, following the "intimate, not generic" discipline. No `apcs_reliability_profile`; rules reference specific discerner outputs by name
- Update `apcs_reconciliation` in APCS0 from stub to authoritative
- Name any new quoins the rules require (candidates: `apcs_corroboration`, `apcs_subsumption`, `apcs_role`, `apcs_disposition`, `apcs_clinician_decision`)
- Cascade changes through APCS0 as decisions crystallize — finding outputs may gain fields; `apcs_unified_classification` receives its concrete shape

## Expected Concrete Rules (Starting Set, Open to Redirect)

These are hypotheses for the next session, not commitments:

- `apcs_regex_finding` is authoritative for its category — no corroboration needed, no contradiction admitted
- `apcs_stanford_finding(PATIENT)` overlapping a Patient-label `apcs_label_finding` → unified as the phrase-scoped finding (granularity merger)
- `apcs_dictionary_finding` uncorroborated by `apcs_stanford_finding` or an `apcs_label_finding` → surfaced for clinician decision
- `apcs_city_finding` from dictionary corroborated by `apcs_stanford_finding(HOSPITAL)` → unified as FACILITY (HOSPITAL subsumes CITY)
- Category authority table: per-category rule for which discerner wins when discerners disagree

## Non-goals

- No code changes to Rust sources (the spec drives code, not the other way around — code changes come in a later pace once APCS0 is authoritative)
- No changes to APCAS, APCPS, or `apck-claude-context.md` unless a cascade from APCS0 makes them incorrect
- No changes to the four discerners themselves — their outputs are the inputs to reconciliation, not the subject of this pace

## Exit Criteria

- `== Reconciliation` section in APCS0 is authoritative (no DEFERRED flags anywhere)
- Terminology for role/disposition is chosen, documented, and voiced in the right AXLA motifs
- Dictionary-finding schema is decided and expressed in the `apcs_dictionary_finding` quoin
- Clinician-toggle composition is decided and expressed as a quoin or as a rule
- Every rule references specific discerner outputs by name — no generic abstractions
- A reader can trace the complete path from `apcs_normalized_text` through each discerner, through reconciliation, to `apcs_unified_classification`, and on to anonymized output — no gaps, no hand-waving
- `tt/apcw-t.Test.sh` still passes (the spec change is not code, but no cascade should break tests)

### rust-container-integration (₢A9AAa) [rough]

## Character

Rust code wiring the existing Clipbuddy focus handler to the container's bind-mount interface. Not architectural — APCS0 and APCPS already pin every contract this pace consumes. Sonnet-scale, mostly mechanical plumbing with one interesting beat (graceful degradation when the container isn't up).

## Prerequisites

₢A9AAZ (`container-scaffolding`) must land first — the container has to exist and produce valid `{N}.json` before Rust can integrate with it. Smoke-test validation of the container is the entry gate for this pace.

## Context

A9AAV decided: Rust Clipbuddy stays the frontend; sophisticated algorithms live in the container; combining stays in Rust (strongly typed). ₢A9AAZ constructs the container in isolation. This pace builds the Rust half: write `{N}-in.txt`, watch for `{N}.json`, deserialize, log. No combining yet — this pace proves the loop, the next pace (combining-v1, deferred) does the intelligence once empirical evidence is in hand.

## Scope — Container Integration Module
